appIs.value: tic-tac-toe — a two-player m,n,k (3,3,3) game served as a web arena

goal.value: place three of your own marks in a row (horizontal, vertical, or diagonal); otherwise draw

howToJoin.value: take a seat by submitting the first move for a side; the first mover on each side is seated (X moves first, then O); later arrivals are observers

myAffordances: make a move on X's turn to an empty square

expectedActionVocabSource.note: A=0 puts a form on every square, so the board names the vocabulary; /profile enumerates it too. C makes the vocabulary explicit on the board via aria-labels ('{position}, {state}').

expectedArtifactDiscovery: ARIA grid/gridcell roles + a live status region; squares accessibly labeled '{position}, {state}' — the position vocabulary (TopLeft..BottomRight) exposed to assistive tech
the /profile ALPS contract (advertised via Link: rel=profile) enumerating make-move (player X/O + the 9 named positions); Allow/OPTIONS on the game; /.well-known/home
schema.org Game JSON-LD typing (Link: rel=describedby → /games/{id}/type; ld+json conneg 303s the game to its describing document)
notes: Factors on: CSdSo. A=0: a live move form on EVERY square (naive design) — occupied and out-of-turn squares included, none disabled; the affordance is ungated. C=1: accessible structure; position vocabulary announced via aria-labels. Sd=1: /profile ALPS contract + Link/Allow/OPTIONS headers expose the move format. So=1: schema.org Game ontology typing.
